title: Bug: FusedMemoryConfig defaults assume orchestrator/ cwd
analysis:
FusedMemoryConfig has three defaults that only work when cwd is the orchestrator/ subdirectory:

1. `url` defaults to `http://localhost:8000` — dark-factory uses port 8002 (port scheme: reify=xxx0, autopilot-video=xxx1, dark-factory=xxx2). During the dashboard run, this caused the orchestrator to connect to reify's server on port 8000 and see 0 pending tasks.

2. `config_path` defaults to `../fused-memory/config/config.yaml` — parent-relative path that only resolves correctly from orchestrator/ dir. McpLifecycle resolves this as (project_root / config_path).resolve(), so with project_root=repo-root it becomes repo-root/../fused-memory/... which escapes the repo.

3. `server_command` defaults with `--project ../fused-memory` — same parent-relative issue. McpLifecycle runs server_command with cwd=project_root, so ../fused-memory resolves above repo root.

The real config at `orchestrator/config.yaml` already has correct values (url=8002, repo-root-relative paths), but load_config without --config only looks for `config.yaml` in cwd. When cwd is repo root (common case), there's no config.yaml there — YamlSettingsSource returns {} and all (wrong) defaults apply.

Secondary issue: `settings_customise_sources` defaults to loading `config.yaml` from cwd. When no --config is passed and no config.yaml exists in cwd, it silently uses all defaults with no warning.

Fix approach:
- Change FusedMemoryConfig defaults to be repo-root-relative (matching McpLifecycle's resolution: config_path relative to project_root, server_command run with cwd=project_root)
- Add model_validator to resolve project_root to absolute path (prevents cwd-sensitivity after construction)
- Add config file auto-discovery in load_config to search orchestrator/config.yaml when cwd/config.yaml not found
- Log warning when no config file found at any standard location

Downstream consumers need no changes — mcp_lifecycle.py already resolves config_path against project_root (line 233) and runs server_command with cwd=project_root (line 239). The existing test test_fused_memory_defaults asserts old broken defaults and must be updated.

steps.description: Update test_fused_memory_defaults to assert correct repo-root-relative defaults: url='http://localhost:8002', config_path='fused-memory/config/config.yaml' (no ../), server_command containing '--project' followed by 'fused-memory' (no ../). Add assertion that no element in server_command contains '../'. This test will FAIL because current defaults have port 8000 and parent-relative paths.

Fix FusedMemoryConfig defaults in config.py: (1) url from 'http://localhost:8000' to 'http://localhost:8002', (2) config_path from '../fused-memory/config/config.yaml' to 'fused-memory/config/config.yaml', (3) server_command '--project' arg from '../fused-memory' to 'fused-memory'.

Write test_project_root_resolved_to_absolute in TestDefaults: create OrchestratorConfig with default project_root=Path('.'), assert config.project_root.is_absolute(). Write test_fused_memory_paths_resolve_under_project_root in a new TestPathResolution class: create config with project_root=tmp_path, verify (project_root / config.fused_memory.config_path).resolve() starts with str(tmp_path), verify no '..' segments remain after resolve. Both tests FAIL because no model_validator exists yet.

Add model_validator(mode='after') on OrchestratorConfig that resolves project_root to absolute: self.project_root = self.project_root.resolve(). Import model_validator from pydantic. This ensures all downstream path joins work regardless of cwd changes after config construction.

Write test_load_config_discovers_orchestrator_subdir in TestYamlLoading: create tmp_path/orchestrator/config.yaml with max_concurrent_tasks=7, monkeypatch chdir to tmp_path and clear ORCH_CONFIG_PATH, call load_config(None), assert config.max_concurrent_tasks == 7. This tests auto-discovery of orchestrator/config.yaml from repo root — will FAIL because load_config only checks cwd/config.yaml.

Add _find_config(explicit_path: Path | None) -> Path | None helper to config.py. Search order: (1) explicit_path if given, (2) ORCH_CONFIG_PATH env var path if it exists, (3) cwd/config.yaml, (4) cwd/orchestrator/config.yaml. Update load_config to call _find_config and set ORCH_CONFIG_PATH when found.

Write test_load_config_warns_when_no_config_found in TestYamlLoading: monkeypatch chdir to empty tmp_path, clear ORCH_CONFIG_PATH env var, call load_config(None), use caplog fixture to assert a WARNING-level message containing 'No config file found' is logged. Will FAIL because no warning logic exists yet.

Add warning log in load_config when _find_config returns None and no explicit path was given. Message: 'No config file found (checked config.yaml, orchestrator/config.yaml). Using defaults. Pass --config or set ORCH_CONFIG_PATH to specify.'

design_decisions.decision: Change url default to http://localhost:8002 (dark-factory port)
design_decisions.rationale: The port scheme documented in orchestrator/config.yaml is: reify=xxx0, autopilot-video=xxx1, dark-factory=xxx2. Port 8000 collides with reify and caused the actual bug (connecting to wrong project's server, seeing 0 pending tasks). Since this is the dark-factory orchestrator, its defaults should match the dark-factory port. The fused-memory config.yaml also defines port: 8002.

design_decisions.decision: Fix defaults to repo-root-relative paths (Option 1) rather than adding complex path rewriting in a model_validator (pure Option 3)
design_decisions.rationale: mcp_lifecycle.py already resolves config_path against project_root (line 233) and runs server_command with cwd=project_root (line 239). The bug is simply that defaults use '../' which assumes orchestrator/ as cwd. Changing defaults to repo-root-relative is the minimal correct fix. A model_validator that rewrites FusedMemoryConfig paths would duplicate logic already in mcp_lifecycle.

design_decisions.decision: Add model_validator to resolve project_root to absolute (light Option 3 addition)
design_decisions.rationale: project_root defaults to Path('.') which is relative and context-dependent. Making it absolute in a model_validator ensures all downstream path joins (config_path, server_command cwd, escalation queue_dir) work correctly regardless of cwd changes after config construction. This is a 2-line addition with high defensive value.

The standard config file lives at orchestrator/config.yaml. When running 'orchestrator run' from repo root (common case), load_config should find it automatically rather than silently using defaults. This directly addresses the task's secondary concern about settings_customise_sources silently using all defaults when no config.yaml exists in cwd.

design_decisions.decision: Warn on missing config rather than fail
design_decisions.rationale: Failing would be a breaking change for scripts that rely on defaults or env vars (ORCH_ prefix). A warning is the right balance: it surfaces the problem (silent defaults) without breaking anything, and points the user to the fix (--config or ORCH_CONFIG_PATH).

reuse.how: Update the existing test to assert new correct defaults instead of writing a separate test. This keeps the test suite clean and avoids two tests checking the same thing.

McpLifecycle already resolves config_path as (project_root / config_path).resolve() and runs server_command with cwd=project_root. New defaults align with this existing resolution — no changes needed in mcp_lifecycle.py.
The _find_config helper works upstream of YamlSettingsSource — it locates the right path and sets ORCH_CONFIG_PATH for YamlSettingsSource to consume. No changes needed to YamlSettingsSource itself.
New config discovery and warning tests follow the same pattern: create config files under tmp_path, call load_config, assert values. Add monkeypatch for chdir since discovery depends on cwd.
Use @model_validator(mode='after') on OrchestratorConfig to resolve project_root. Pydantic 2.x model_validator is the standard approach for post-processing constructed models.
